mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in labor market conditions signals impending corporate revenue contraction, as rising unemployment reduces consumer spending power and forces earnings downgrades across cyclical sectors.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal exhibits no predictive power in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in housing construction and consumer credit indicate a synchronized demand shock that compresses corporate revenues, driving negative equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 900 and RSAFS declines > 3% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:July 2008 housing starts at 965k, credit outstanding at $381T
failure_condition: Joint HOUST and RSAFS declines fail to predict negative 60-day forward returns more than 55% of the time, or the interaction term shows zero statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production following a supply disruption confirms capacity restoration and improved logistics, which reduces input cost inflation and lifts industrial sector earnings, driving short-term equity gains.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO prints consistently coincide with negative 5-day forward returns, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating quarterly real GDP growth reflects expanding aggregate demand, which directly increases corporate profit margins and drives sustained positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP acceleration fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns in more than 40% of observations, or the signal's information coefficient decays to zero after controlling for FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding 4% forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, increasing the equity risk premium and compressing valuation multiples, which drives negative short-term market returns.
failure_condition: The signal predicts negative returns in fewer than 45% of triggered instances, or forward returns remain statistically indistinguishable from zero during high-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll deceleration and rising unemployment confirm a structural labor market cooling that precedes broader economic contraction, triggering systematic deleveraging and negative equity performance.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS and UNRATE signal exhibits a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term fails to improve predictive accuracy over single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Negative quarterly GDP growth confirms a technical recession that forces corporate earnings revisions downward and triggers risk-off portfolio rebalancing, producing severe negative equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Negative GDP prints fail to predict negative 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of cases, or the signal's directional accuracy does not significantly exceed random chance in walk-forward validation.
